Tables.HeaderSignature: terms|definitions
SampleFirstDataRow: This is the safety integrity level "ASIL" (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) for each safety function or system. This is a scale with 5 levels of growing criticality (QM, A, B, C, D) that refines the notion of ER G4. The QM level refers to Quality Management and to the standard ISO-TS 16949. The level results from an analysis that include:- the frequency of the risk situations (E1 to E4)- the driver's ability to control the consequences of the ER (C0 to C3)- the severity of the consequences (S1 to S3)

to list and refine the requirements that have an impact on the <component name> functionality
locate the part in its environment via the cont...
ContentParagraphs: The goal of the present Specification is:
to list and refine the requirements that have an impact on the <component name> functionality
locate the part in its environment via the contextual diagram
to specify the performance, operational, constraint, integration, and validation requirements for <component name>

For each application project, this general-purpose document is used as a reference Specification to the corresponding applicative Specification which specifies the requirements applying to the project in both EMEA and North America markets (for harmonized and/or non-harmonized applications).
<<Or if the applicative Specification is a simple application matrix of the generic Specification>>:
For each application project, this general-purpose document is accompanied by one corresponding application specification specifying the requirements applying to the project.
The objective of this Specification is to:
specify the requirements applicable to <Part name> Part developed for project <name of the Model>, from requirements defined in the generic Specification of the Part <Component name>, <reference>

to specify the values of the performances and of the internal parameters to the generic Part Specification <Component name>, <reference>, applicable to <Part name> Part developed for project <name of the Model>

Headings.Title: DEPENDABILITY VOCABULARY
Headings.ContentPreview:
Term
Definitions
Safety
Capacity of the system to prevent the appearance, under the given conditions, of critical or catastrophic events.
Standard ISO 26262
The Standard ISO 26262 declines for the vehicle, the standard C...
ContentParagraphs: Capacity of the system to prevent the appearance, under the given conditions, of critical or catastrophic events.
The Standard ISO 26262 declines for the vehicle, the standard CEI 61508 that refers to the safety of the electrical and electronic systems
Capacity of the system to perform or to be able to perform the required function at a given moment.
Capacity of a device to perform the required function, in given conditions of use and maintenance, for a given duration (NF X 60-500).
Probability of being defective before a given time, mileage or number of stresses. Unreliability according to a given duration t is the probability of failure in the interval [0, t]:
Intrinsic behavior of a component (or of a system)

This is a matter of the fitness of a component or sub-system to withstand damage. The level of damage before failure that the component or the sub-system is capable of is modelled by a probability strategy (Weibull distribution in most cases, sometimes normal distribution).
Note: Intrinsic behavior is strictly the SPR's liability.
Operating performance of a component (or of a system)

This is a matter of the behavior performances of the component or sub-system used by the customer. These performances result from the intrinsic resistance of the components with their dispersion and the forces levels encountered in customer use.
Note: The supplier brings his view feedback when he has it, but the performance in service is always set by STELLANTIS.

In given conditions of use for which it was designed, the capacity of a system to be maintained or restored to a state where it can perform a required function, when the maintenance is performed in given conditions, using the prescribed procedures and resources.
This is the behavior that leads to a non-desired situation.
The vocabulary of the standard ISO26262 distinguishes between:
which has a physical character that can be altered by a reliability strategy (be it: Weibull, normal, exponential…)
A failure triggered by an external environment itself random is part of this category of breakdowns, if the environment conditions were planned at the moment of the design.
resulting from the nonconformity of the state of the art at the level of design, validation, production... (ex: specification, traceability of requirements, lack of knowledge on the maximum stress)
The development of software without respecting the V cycle is a systematic failure.
The systematic failures are not part of the perimeter discussed by the durability procedure.
Physical phenomenon at the origin of the failure
The functional failure modes refer to the (physical or information) output component flows and are classified as follows:
The continuous lines represent the expected state, and the dotted lines - the actual state
Some information is transmitted, but it does not correspond to a possible valid state
Some information is transmitted; it corresponds to a valid state, but it does not have the expected value.
An error outside tolerance is present on the information.
(*) is mainly applicable to digital signals.
(**) is mainly applied to an analogy signal.
This is a matter of the degradation process that leads to failure after a certain time of use of the vehicle by the customer.

The fatigue under variable load is generally subject to a STELLANTIS specification of the level of "equivalent fatigue" as a resistance to a number of cycles set. The intrinsic resistance under this number of cycles follows a normal strategy.

The fatigue under approximately constant load is the number of rupture cycles which is specified; according to the level of knowledge of the behavior in fatigue, or we can also use a modelling via the Wohler curves, or we can model the resistance distribution by a log-normal strategy.

The fatigue damage level is not measurable in the vehicle: the intrinsic resistance potential can only be evaluated only by tests carried out until rupture (the experience feedback allows us in some cases to avoid it).

The number of cycles with ruptures is modelled by Weibull distribution. The wear level can generally be measured, which allows us to estimate the intrinsic resistance potential by extrapolation if we know the maximum wear level leading to the loss of service.
Failures modelled by an exponential strategy
(random or systematic in conformity with the standard) characterized by an instantaneous failure rate or an average time (distance or stress) between failures.

Steps should be taken to adjust the phenomena of corrosion or degradation in ambient air (ozone for example). The impact of these phenomena is estimated by the SPR as a natural accelerator factor and is communicated to STELLANTIS when identified.

We retain the main failure modes which correspond to the weak links. This means that the other failure modes are (subject to a normal implementation) manifestly impossible to encounter considering the dimensioning and the layout of the studied system.

This is the event after a failure having a negative impact on the customer or his environment. There is the functional ER caused by the failure of a functional output flow of the "threat - damage ER" which is not caused by a failure of the components or the systems functional.

The dreaded events are classified according to their criticality level: The criticality level is an estimate of the effect of the dreaded event considered in terms of inconvenience for the user, of cost for repairing, or of individual risk. The dreaded events are quoted according to the following quotation system:
Dissatisfaction or degradation of vehicle functionality.
General performances of the vehicle preserved. The user can continue to use his vehicle. No immediate intervention required.
Appearance of constraining symptoms. The user can continue to use his vehicle, but a fast intervention is required.
The vehicle is unavailable for the user.
Impossibility to drive or impossibility to reach the destination because of the loss of a main function, or because of non-compliance with the regulations (risk of fine).
Impossibility to park the vehicle as is (risk of inviolability).
Risk of bodily harm for man.
It can potentially lead to a traffic accident or physical injuries.

This is the safety integrity level "ASIL" (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) for each safety function or system. This is a scale with 5 levels of growing criticality (QM, A, B, C, D) that refines the notion of ER G4. The QM level refers to Quality Management and to the standard ISO-TS 16949. The level results from an analysis that include:
- the frequency of the risk situations (E1 to E4)
- the driver's ability to control the consequences of the ER (C0 to C3)
- the severity of the consequences (S1 to S3)
Its application increases the damage and accelerates the failure of the component or sub-system. There is a major interest in accelerating the durability tests.
Note: Such a factor is used if the SPR commits himself to the value of the acceleration coefficient (recognized calculation, experimentation, etc.).
In the presence of ETI when the presence of flames or combustion is not confined to the inside of the equipment and/or when a voluntary intervention is necessary to stop it. cf. definition [STA14].
There are the Parallel ETI and the Series ETI:

Parallel ETI: Insulation fault at constant voltage between + and - (potential difference). If the insulation resistance decreases, the leakage current increases the power increases, and a hot spot (P= U²/R) is created
Series ETI: Rated current or overload conduction fault. If the electrical resistance increases the power increases and a hot spot (P= RI²) is created.
All the technical solutions that prevent the appearance of a "hot spot" in the EE part (critical temperature rise in relation to its environment)
All the technical solutions that prevent the propagation of the hot spot outside the EE part.
Thermal incidents due to excessive local heating from electrical origin leading to ignition temperature of materials. The propagation of a hot spot could lead to an ETI.

Random noise is the emission of sound created:
At the level of the contact area between two parts dur...
ContentParagraphs: Random noise is the noise heard by the customers, random in nature, generated by the road profile forces.
Random noise is the emission of sound created:
At the level of the contact area between two parts during relative movements (slapping, grating…),
By distortion of the parts of tolerances not adapted between parts (rattle, vibration…).
The rating of the defects is made by subjective listening when new and after ageing, which allows us to classify the random noise on a 4 levels scale: A, B, C and D.
Low retouch but degraded brand image
The loudness detection test on a bench, including a climatic and vibration ageing has 3 phases:
listening when new ("human ear" subjective listening) + rating of the levels in dB(A)
listening after ageing ("human ear" subjective listening) + rating of the levels in dB(A)

In all cases, the test means will be submitted for approval to STELLANTIS
The background noise will be lower than 18dB (A) on average (measured in 1/3 octave).
During the organic measurements, the background noise should be lower than 10 dB (A) in relation to the measurement.
The supplier will indicate at what frequencies the difference between the measured noise and the chamber noise is < 10 dB (A). This limit should not be located above 200 Hz.

When handling the <<part / component>> with a remote connection, before and during its mounting on the vehicle, that can be handled by its integrated harness. During handling before and during assembly on the vehicle, the organs can be subject to accidental falls to the ground (concrete). Following this type of shock the part must remain conform.

Mandatory or suggested tests if any provided within this standard are meant to assure compliance to the specification but do not relieve the responsible entity from ensuring that the component or system meets all environmental, physical, electrical, mechanical, functional, regulatory and durability requirements including all applicable system interface requirements as described in the standard for the entire duration of specified useful life.
Mandatory section; keep mandatory wording in black font

A test plan (DVP&R) shall be developed to verify and validate all required functions specified in this standard. The responsible entity shall provide any additional testing that’s deemed necessary to meet functional requirements; the tables at the end of this document provide summary of all the requirements and shall be used to develop the test planning.

Samples utilized for Design Verification (DV) shall meet specified drawings tolerance range. Samples for Production Validation (PV) shall meet statistical capabilities for machine and manufacturing processes.

Where necessary, each line item of the test plan (DVP&R) must be supported by a comprehensive test or laboratory procedure. A test plan (DVP&R) agreement by Stellantis does not relieve the responsible entity from ensuring that the component or system meets all environmental, physical, electrical, mechanical, functional, regulatory and durability requirements including all applicable system interface requirements, in the entity’s area of responsibility, as described in the standard for the specified useful life.

Parametric Evaluation Technique (PET) compares the statistical distribution of the sample population based on desired reliability/ confidence (such as, R95/C90 or R90/C90) to demonstrate conformance to acceptance limits during design validation process. PET analysis shall be done on the identified key functional parameters only.

For PET analysis, samples from all environmental test legs shall be used (up to 35 samples) during DV and PV tests (refer to red pentagon in the test flows in Annex B). These samples shall be measured for key functional parameters before start of any testing (pre-test measurement) and after completion of all tests (post-test measurement) under selected temperature and voltage conditions (either 3, 5, 7 or 9 point checks). Suppliers shall use PET spreadsheet provided by SD-12501) to demonstrate reliability/confidence targets specified in the component specific performance standard. PET charts shall be prepared for every parametric measurement criteria (5 point check will require 5 separate PET charts, and so on).

Three point checks shall be used only for the functional checks (blue diamond in the test flows in Annex B). Nine or seven point checks shall be used for “Safety or Powertrain” applications and at the minimum five point checks shall be used for convenience applications as part of PET analysis at the start and end of the tests (red pentagon in the test flows in Annex B).

Headings.ContentPreview:
This chapter describes what is required of the supplier in terms of proof that <<part / component name>> meets the requirements of § 5.1 to 5.5.
Requirement Number (v)
Description of the requirement
Input requirement (v)...
ContentParagraphs: This chapter describes what is required of the supplier in terms of proof that <<part / component name>> meets the requirements of § 5.1 to 5.5.

To demonstrate that the requirements of § 5.1 to 5.5 are met the supplier proposes detailed validation tests in its Validation Plan. These are organized into detailed test files to optimize the stress and / or stress sequences according to the planned test times, to respect the development schedule.
The Validation Plan is controlled by STELLANTIS at each fixing milestone of the part definition.

If the part is sensitive to thermal ageing, the supplier shall justify the durability, by design justification or/and by leading high temperature endurance tests (example: CL10 of [STLA standard [N43]]).
The thermal ageing is due to thermal and functional constraints.
The activation endurance tests shall be sized according to:
- the failure rate of the paragraphs DEPENDABILITY (Safety) (§ 5.4.4) and Product quality (§ 5.4.5),
- the number of samples in test,
- the thermal acceleration factor concerning the ageing model of the part for the relative failure mode.

If the activations of the part causes an ageing, the supplier shall justify the durability, by design justification or/and by leading activation endurance tests (example: CL09 of [STLA standard [N43]]).
The ageing caused by activations, may be:
- Mechanical wears (mechatronic components like pushes, contacts, starters…)
- Thermal stress (electronic components sensitive to temperature variations due to electrical activations, like IGBT, MOS, capacitors…)
The activation endurance tests shall be sized according to:
- the failure rate of the paragraphs DEPENDABILITY (Safety) (§ 5.4.4) and Product quality (§ 5.4.5),
- the number of samples in test,

- the ageing model of the part for the relative failure mode. If we consider a Weibull distribution of the failures with beta = 5 (dispersion hypothesis), the following requirement are applicable. Otherwise, the supplier shall give his level of knowledge in term of laws of ageing for each mode of collapse (fatigue, wear…).
